name: Bill's Sourdough French Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Feed your starter at least 12 hours before making bread.
2. Combine 2 1/2 cups of flour, yeast, sugar, and salt in a large bowl. Add water, oil, and starter; beat with an electric mixer for 3 minutes.
3. Work additional flour into the dough by hand until it can be handled. The amount will vary depending on the consistency of the starter used.
4. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured board and knead until smooth, satiny, and elastic, about 10 minutes, adding more flour as needed to prevent sticking to the board or hands.
5. Place the dough in a large oiled bowl, turning once to coat the dough ball; an oil-based cooking spray works well for this. Cover with plastic wrap and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 to 1 1/2 hours.
6. Punch down the dough and divide into either 2 or 4 pieces based on loaf size desired. Cover and let rest 10 minutes.
7. Roll each piece out into a rectangle and roll up from the long side, pinching the seam closed and tapering the ends. Place the loaves, seam side down, in baguette pans or on an oiled baking sheet sprinkled with cornmeal.
8. Make quarter-inch deep slashes along the length or diagonally every 2-3 inches on each loaf.
9. Beat egg white with 1 tablespoon water until frothy and brush the loaves with it. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
10. Bake at 375°F for 20 minutes, then brush again with egg white and bake an additional 20 minutes.
11. Remove from pans and cool on wire racks.
